content: I'm at a crossroads with my freelance graphic design career. I'm 62 and recently adopted a golden retriever named Coco from the shelter. I'm wondering if I should scale back my work.
That's wonderful about Coco! How has having a dog changed your daily routine and work schedule?

It's been great but definitely more structured. I need to be home for walks and playtime. I used to take on as many projects as I could, but now I'm more selective. I'm also thinking about my travel blogging hobby.
Travel blogging sounds like a fun passion project. How does that fit with your current workload and Coco's needs?

Well, that's part of the problem. I love combining my photography with travel writing, but it's hard to do that when I'm booked with client work. I'm also really into salsa dancing and miss having weeknight free for classes.
It sounds like you have a lot of interests competing for your time. Have you thought about specializing in a niche area of graphic design that might give you more flexibility?
I've considered it. I'm really good at branding for travel and lifestyle companies, which would align with my blogging. But I'm worried about turning down other work and reducing my income.
What does your financial situation look like? Are you in a position to potentially earn less if it means more lifestyle flexibility?

I've been freelancing for 20 years, so I've built up good savings. Coco's adoption fee was $350 and monthly expenses are about $80 for food and supplies. I could probably manage on less if I budget carefully.
That's helpful context. How do you feel about potentially working part-time or taking on retainer clients instead of project work?
I like the retainer idea, especially if I could find clients in the travel industry. I could still do my leatherworking on the side - I make wallets and bags that I sometimes sell at craft fairs.
It sounds like you're leaning toward a more balanced approach. Have you talked to any of your current clients about changing your arrangement?
Not yet, but I think I'm going to propose a retainer model to my top three clients next month. I want to make sure Coco and I have a stable routine before I make any big changes.
